items.headline: Global Stocks Tumble Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Tech Sell-off
items.summary: Global shares experienced a significant decline on Monday, with Wall Street indices sinking, as escalating conflict between Israel and Iran, coupled with mounting skepticism surrounding high-valuation technology stocks, dampened investor sentiment. Oil prices surged higher on renewed Middle East instability. A strong U.S. jobs report also fueled expectations for potential Federal Reserve interest rate hikes, adding to market uncertainty.

items.headline: Nurix Therapeutics Soars on Licensing Deal
items.summary: Nurix Therapeutics shares skyrocketed after the biotech announced a global licensing and collaboration agreement with Roche, valued at up to $2.3 billion, for bexobrutideg. Nurix will receive a $700 million upfront payment.

items.headline: Cooper Companies Posts Strong Earnings
items.summary: The Cooper Companies Inc. jumped after reporting better-than-expected second-quarter fiscal 2026 adjusted earnings per share.

items.headline: ABM Industries Climbs on Revenue Beat
items.summary: Shares of ABM Industries Inc. climbed after the company posted second-quarter fiscal 2026 revenues that outpaced analyst estimates.

items.headline: Above Food Plummets on NASDAQ Letter
items.summary: Above Food Ingredients Inc. shares experienced a drastic decline following a NASDAQ determination letter under listing rules.

items.headline: Jianzhi Education Group Dives After Offering
items.summary: Jianzhi Education Technology Group Company Limited American depositary shares fell sharply after the announcement of the pricing of a $5 million registered direct offering.

items.headline: Broadcom Drops on AI Outlook
items.summary: Broadcom shares dropped significantly after its artificial intelligence outlook failed to meet elevated market expectations.

items.headline: Nvidia Leads Tech Sector Downslide
items.summary: Nvidia, a bellwether for AI-linked technology, fell sharply amidst a broader sell-off in the technology sector driven by AI skepticism and high valuations.

items.headline: Honeywell Slips Despite Reaffirming Guidance
items.summary: Honeywell shares slipped after the industrial conglomerate reaffirmed its full-year 2026 sales and adjusted EPS guidance, while also providing an outlook for its 'Honeywell Technologies' entity post-spin-off.

items.headline: Oil Prices Surge as Iran-Israel Conflict Escalates
items.summary: Brent crude jumped over $4 to $97.69 a barrel, and benchmark U.S. crude surged to $94.67 a barrel, as fighting flared between Israel and Iran, leading to airstrikes and renewed fears of supply disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Tech Sector Leads Market Decline Amid AI Doubts
items.summary: The technology sector bore the brunt of Monday's sell-off, with major tech-heavy indices like the Nasdaq Composite slumping significantly. Investors are showing increased skepticism about the rally in AI-linked shares, especially after some companies' AI outlooks failed to meet high market expectations.

items.headline: Strong Jobs Report Fuels Fed Rate Hike Expectations
items.summary: A stronger-than-expected U.S. jobs report for May, showing 172,000 new positions, has reinforced market expectations that the Federal Reserve may consider raising interest rates this year. Attention now turns to the upcoming CPI data release this week for further clues on inflation and monetary policy direction.

items.headline: Campbell's Reports Q3 Fiscal 2026 Results, Sales Down
items.summary: The Campbell's Company ($CPB) reported a 4% decrease in net sales and a 32% decline in adjusted EPS for its third quarter fiscal 2026. The company reaffirmed its full-year fiscal 2026 guidance, noting that results were in line with expectations but faced top-line softness and inflation-driven margin headwinds.

items.headline: Japan Revises Q1 Economic Growth Downward
items.summary: Japan's government revised its annualized economic growth rate for the first quarter of this year to 1.8%, a decrease from an earlier estimate of 2.1%. This adjustment reflects weaker-than-expected capital spending.
